reasoning: Stock returns are extremely difficult to predict over short time horizons. Comcast (CMCSA) has exhibited significant negative momentum in 2025, falling from around $32 in July to below $27 by late October due to business struggles, like broadband customer losses and increased competition as seen in recent earnings news. Over the shortest horizon (1 week out to 2025-11-02), this recent negative trend exerts some downward pressure on the stock, producing a slightly below-50% baseline chance of the price being higher than the 2025-10-26 reference point. As the forecast horizon extends respectively to November 25, 2025 and January 24, 2026, the noise of daily / weekly price movements increasingly dominates and technical signals wash out. In strict compliance with the explicit instruction that uncertainty should increase and probabilities should regress closer to 0.5 over longer horizons, my estimates drift steadily back to the 50% baseline.
